MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: LAWRENCE MANN, ESQ., BOURHIS & MANN, 415.392.4660, FOR PLAINTIFF MIGUEL PALMA.
LINDA LAWSON, ESQ., MESERVE, MUMPER & HUGHES, 213.620.0300, FOR DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted as to the misrepresentation and intentional infliction of emotional distress causes of action. THE COURT TAKES UNDER SUBMISSION DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION as to the bad faith cause of action and request for punitive damages. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued to October 11, 2012 because the Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant shall immediately lodge a courtesy copy of the moving papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Anne Michelle Burr of the Office of the Attorney General (703.1403) for defendant and moving party; Christopher S. Milligan of Meserve Mumper & hughes, LLP (213.620.0300) appearing via Court Call for defendant Prudential; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff again fails to allege that the defendant violated a specific mandatory duty. A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also, once again, fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KAREN YIU, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5385 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT INSURANCE COMMISSIONER;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER MILLIGAN OF MESERVE, MUMPER & HUGHES, LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 620-0300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PRUDENTIAL BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572) A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)
